title: Multi-product attribute combinations
summary: Run a market simulation for every combination (cartesian product or zip) of attribute values across multiple products.
description: Generates multiple simulation points by combining the value lists in scenario_chgs across all products and attributes, then runs each point through the choice simulator. The result is a stacked CSV suitable for price-grid or feature-matrix analyses. Use expand_grid = true (default) for a full cartesian product, or expand_grid = false to zip the lists together in parallel.
usage:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_combinations"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
measures = "share|revenue"
expand_grid = true

["cs::sim_combinations".scenario_chgs.ProductA]
price = [9.0, 10.0, 11.0]

["cs::sim_combinations".scenario_chgs.ProductB]
price = [14.0, 15.0, 16.0]

arguments.name: measures
arguments.description: Pipe-separated output measures. Any subset of: share, revenue, volume, cost, profit, standard_error. Use "__all__" for all six.

arguments.name: scenarios
arguments.description: Names of scenarios to run. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario in the sim_db.

arguments.name: subgroups
arguments.description: Subgroup column names. Use ["__all__"] for every subgroup column.

arguments.name: weights
arguments.description: Weight column names. Use ["__all__"] for all weights.

arguments.name: throttles
arguments.description: Throttle-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: considerations
arguments.description: Consideration-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: include_nets
arguments.description: When true, append net rows summed by product_net group.

arguments.name: expand_grid
arguments.description: When true (default), compute the full cartesian product of all value lists across all products and attributes, producing one simulation point per unique combination. When false, zip the lists together in parallel (stopping at the shortest list length), producing one simulation point per position.

arguments.name: exponents
arguments.description: Beta scale factor applied before exponentiation.

arguments.name: prob_methods
arguments.description: Probability computation method(s). Single method or a pipe-separated blend (e.g. "sop|top2"). Methods: "sop" — standard share-of-preference (multinomial logit); "topN" where N ≥ 1 — top-N restricted MNL, only the N highest-utility products receive non-zero probability ("top1" is equivalent to first choice); "<top_spec>:<bot_spec>-<att>" — two-level nested logit where products are grouped by scenario attribute <att>, with <bot_spec> within groups and <top_spec> across groups (e.g. "sop:sop-Brand", "top1:sop-Category"); "<bot_spec>-<att>" shorthand sets top_spec=sop implicitly. Blend methods with | and weights via prob_methods_wgts.

arguments.name: trips
arguments.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Must be >= 1.

arguments.name: price_formulas
arguments.description: Eval-lang expressions for derived prices, applied before each combination point.

arguments.name: scenario_chgs
arguments.description: Attribute value lists keyed by product_name. Arrays define the axis of values to combine. Example: {ProductA: {price: [9, 10, 11]}, ProductB: {price: [14, 15, 16]}}.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Rayon worker threads for the points × scenarios × weights fan-out. `1` (default) runs serially. Each combination point is scored independently, so parallel speedup scales with the size of the grid. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

details:
All products' value lists in scenario_chgs are flattened into a list of axes: [(product, attribute, [values])]. The expand_grid mode builds the cartesian product of all axes; the zip mode pairs position 0 of each axis together, position 1 together, etc., stopping at the shortest axis.

For a grid over two products with three prices each, expand_grid = true produces 3 × 3 = 9 simulation points. zip mode (expand_grid = false) produces 3 points: one per position in the parallel lists.

Scenario labels in the output encode the combination: "base|ProductA|price|9.00|ProductB|price|14.00".

For uniform lock-step movement of all products, sim_lock_step is more concise and makes the intent explicit.
value: Writes one CSV file (sim_combinations_0.csv). Scenario column encodes the combination point. In protocol mode emits event.run.started, event.artifact.created, event.run.finished.
notes: expand_grid = true can produce a very large result set. Two products with 10 prices each yields 100 simulation points; three products, 1000.
For lock-step parallel movement of all products by the same deltas, sim_lock_step is more concise.
In zip mode, all lists should be the same length; the shortest list determines the number of points.

see_also.description: One attribute at a time sensitivity sweep.
Parallel movement of all products by the same delta values.

examples.title: Price grid (2 products, cartesian)
examples.body:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_combinations"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
measures = "share|revenue"
expand_grid = true

["cs::sim_combinations".scenario_chgs.ProductA]
price = [9.0, 10.0, 11.0]

["cs::sim_combinations".scenario_chgs.ProductB]
price = [14.0, 15.0, 16.0]

examples.title: Parallel price movement (zip)
examples.body:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_combinations"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
measures = "share|revenue"
expand_grid = false

["cs::sim_combinations".scenario_chgs.ProductA]
price = [9.0, 10.0, 11.0]

["cs::sim_combinations".scenario_chgs.ProductB]
price = [14.0, 15.0, 16.0]

examples.title: Feature matrix (2 binary features, cartesian)
examples.body:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_combinations"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
measures = "share"
expand_grid = true

["cs::sim_combinations".scenario_chgs.ProductA]
feature_x = [0, 1]
feature_y = [0, 1]

fields.name: scenarios
fields.description: Scenario name(s) to run. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario.

fields.name: subgroups
fields.description: Subgroup column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: weights
fields.description: Weight column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: throttles
fields.description: Throttle set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: considerations
fields.description: Consideration set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: include_nets
fields.description: Add net rows summed by product_net group. Default: false.

fields.name: expand_grid
fields.description: Cartesian product (true) or zip (false). Default: true.

fields.name: exponents
fields.description: Beta scale factor applied before exponentiation. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: prob_methods
fields.description: Probability method: sop, topN (N≥1), nested <top>:<bot>-<att>, or pipe-blended. Default: sop.

fields.name: trips
fields.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Default: 1.

fields.name: trip_seeds
fields.description: Random seed for trip simulation. Default: 100.

fields.name: return_debug
fields.description: Include per-respondent debug output. Default: false.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Rayon worker threads for points × scenarios × weights fan-out. Default: 1.
